form_data.fundingSource: To be finalized based on departmental schemes or new project allocations.
form_data.Expected benefits:
Automated real-time detection of suspicious movement patterns such as route deviations, ghost shipments, circular trips, or repeated invoice usage.

State-wise and sector-wise heatmaps showing goods movement behavior, trend shifts, and anomaly hotspots.

Stronger compliance by identifying potential under-invoicing, over-invoicing, or fraudulent trips.

Reduction in tax evasion and revenue leakage through early anomaly detection.

Better departmental decision-making using ML-powered risk scoring for goods movement.
form_data.Idea Description:
This system analyzes the movement of goods across states, sectors, and taxpayers using multiple datasets including e-way bills, GPS data, and GST filings (GSTR-1).
The system focuses on identifying unusual or suspicious activities by:

Monitoring route patterns and flagging route deviations or unexpected stops.

Detecting anomalies such as ghost shipments, duplicate trips, repeated invoice use, and invoice-movement mismatches.

Integrating GPS traces and e-way bill records to compute route compliance scores using ML models.

Cross-verifying movement data with GSTR-1 declarations to detect discrepancies in invoices vs. actual movement.

Providing dashboards with alerts, visual heatmaps, trip histories, and anomaly explanations.
form_data.potentail Challenges:
Integration with existing departmental platforms such as FO Portal and E-Way Bill Portal.

Ensuring full compliance with DPDP Act, 2023 for data privacy.

Maintaining confidentiality as per CGST/KSGST Act (Section 158).

GPS-based anomaly detection accuracy may vary depending on signal quality, coverage, and device reliability.

Handling IPR concerns and securing model logic from external misuse.
